title: NVIDIA Rubin Platform Launches: Next Generation AI Supercomputer
content: NVIDIA today kickstarted the next generation of AI with the launch of the NVIDIA Rubin platform, comprising six new chips designed to deliver one incredible AI supercomputer. The Rubin platform uses extreme codesign across the six chips — the NVIDIA Vera CPU, NVIDIA Rubin GPU, NVIDIA NVLink 6 Switch, NVIDIA ConnectX-9 SuperNIC, NVIDIA BlueField-4 DPU and NVIDIA Spectrum-6 Ethernet Switch — to slash training time and inference token costs. The Rubin platform features the NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 rack-scale solution and the NVIDIA HGX Rubin NVL8 system. Named for Vera Florence Cooper Rubin — the trailblazing American astronomer whose discoveries transformed humanity's understanding of the universe — the Rubin platform features five major innovations including the latest generations of NVIDIA NVLink interconnect technology, Transformer Engine, Confidential Computing and RAS Engine, as well as the NVIDIA Vera CPU. These breakthroughs will accelerate agentic AI, advanced reasoning and massive-scale mixture-of-experts (MoE) model inference at up to 10x lower cost per token of the NVIDIA Blackwell platform. Compared with its predecessor, the NVIDIA Rubin platform trains MoE models with 4x fewer GPUs to accelerate AI adoption. The Rubin platform harnesses extreme codesign across hardware and software to deliver up to 10x reduction in inference token cost and 4x reduction in number of GPUs to train MoE models, compared with the NVIDIA Blackwell platform. NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet Photonics switch systems deliver 5x improved power efficiency and uptime. A new NVIDIA Inference Context Memory Storage Platform with NVIDIA BlueField-4 storage processor accelerates agentic AI reasoning. Microsoft's next-generation Fairwater AI superfactories — featuring NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 rack-scale systems — will scale to hundreds of thousands of NVIDIA Vera Rubin Superchips. CoreWeave will integrate NVIDIA Rubin-based systems into its AI cloud platform beginning in the second half of 2026. An expanded collaboration with Red Hat delivers a complete AI stack optimized for the Rubin platform with Red Hat Enterprise Linux, Red Hat OpenShift and Red Hat AI. NVIDIA Rubin is in full production, and Rubin-based products will be available from partners the second half of 2026. Among the world's leading AI labs, cloud service providers, computer makers and startups expected to adopt Rubin are Amazon Web Services (AWS), Anthropic, Black Forest Labs, Cisco, Cohere, CoreWeave, Cursor, Dell Technologies, Google, Harvey, HPE, Lambda, Lenovo, Meta, Microsoft, Mistral AI, Nebius, Nscale, OpenAI, OpenEvidence, Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI), Perplexity, Runway, Supermicro, Thinking Machines Lab and xAI.
summary: NVIDIA unveils Rubin platform with six new AI chips offering 10x lower inference token cost and 4x fewer GPUs needed for training MoE models. Full production beginning H2 2026.

title: NVIDIA Releases New Physical AI Models and Robots
content: NVIDIA announced new open models, frameworks and AI infrastructure for physical AI, and unveiled robots for every industry from global partners. Global industry leaders including Boston Dynamics, Caterpillar, Franka Robotics, Humanoid, LG Electronics and NEURA Robotics are using the NVIDIA robotics stack to debut new AI-driven robots. NVIDIA releases new NVIDIA Cosmos and GR00T open models and data for robot learning and reasoning, Isaac Lab-Arena for robot evaluation and the OSMO edge-to-cloud compute framework to simplify robot training workflows. New Open Models include: NVIDIA Cosmos Transfer 2.5 — open, fully customizable world models that enable physically based synthetic data generation and robot policy evaluation in simulation for physical AI. NVIDIA Cosmos Predict 2.5 — an open reasoning vision language model (VLM) that enables intelligent machines to see, understand and act in the physical world like humans. NVIDIA Cosmos Reason 2 — an open reasoning vision language action (VLA) model, purpose-built for humanoid robots, that unlocks full body control and uses NVIDIA Cosmos Reason for better reasoning and contextual understanding. NVIDIA Isaac GR00T N1.6 — Franka Robotics, NEURA Robotics and Humanoid are using GR00T-enabled workflows to simulate, train and validate new behaviors for robots. Salesforce is using Agentforce, Cosmos Reason and the NVIDIA Blueprint for video search and summarization to analyze video footage captured by its robots and reduce incident resolution times by 2x. The new NVIDIA Jetson T4000 module brings the NVIDIA Blackwell architecture to autonomous machines and general robotics for $1,999 at 1,000-unit volume. It delivers 4x the performance of the previous generation with 1,200 FP4 TFLOPS and 64GB of memory, all within a configurable 70-watt envelope ideal for energy-constrained autonomy. NVIDIA IGX Thor will be available later this month, extending robotics to the industrial edge, offering high-performance AI computing with enterprise software support and functional safety. Partners including AAEON, Advantech, ADLINK, Aetina, AVerMedia, Connect Tech, EverFocus, ForeCR, Lanner, RealTimes, Syslogic, Vecow and YUAN offer Thor-powered systems equipped for edge AI, robotics and embedded applications. NEURA Robotics is launching a Porsche-designed Gen 3 humanoid, as well as a smaller-sized humanoid optimized for dexterous control. Richtech Robotics is launching Dex, a mobile humanoid for sophisticated manipulation and navigation across complex industrial environments. AGIBOT is introducing humanoids for both industrial and consumer sectors. LG Electronics unveiled a new home robot built to perform a wide range of indoor household tasks.
summary: NVIDIA announces breakthrough physical AI models and frameworks enabling robots from Boston Dynamics, Caterpillar, LG and others to learn and reason about the physical world. Open models released on Hugging Face.

title: NVIDIA Announces Alpamayo Open-Source AI Models for Autonomous Vehicles
content: NVIDIA unveiled the NVIDIA Alpamayo family of open AI models, simulation tools and datasets designed to accelerate the next era of safe, reasoning-based autonomous vehicle (AV) development. AVs must safely operate across an enormous range of driving conditions, and the rare, complex scenarios, often called the 'long tail,' remain some of the toughest challenges for autonomous systems to safely master. Traditional AV architectures separate perception and planning, which can limit scalability when new or unusual situations arise. Recent advances in end-to-end learning have made significant progress, but overcoming these long-tail edge cases requires models that can safely reason about cause and effect. The Alpamayo family introduces chain-of-thought, reasoning-based vision language action (VLA) models that bring humanlike thinking to AV decision-making. These systems can think through novel or rare scenarios step by step, improving driving capability and explainability — which is critical to scaling trust and safety in intelligent vehicles — and are underpinned by the NVIDIA Halos safety system. At CES, NVIDIA released: Alpamayo 1 — The industry's first chain-of-thought reasoning VLA model designed for the AV research community, now on Hugging Face. With a 10-billion-parameter architecture, Alpamayo 1 uses video input to generate trajectories alongside reasoning traces, showing the logic behind each decision. Developers can adapt Alpamayo 1 into smaller runtime models for vehicle development, or use it as a foundation for AV development tools such as reasoning-based evaluators and auto-labeling systems. AlpaSim — A fully open-source, end-to-end simulation framework for high-fidelity AV development, available on GitHub. It provides realistic sensor modeling, configurable traffic dynamics and scalable closed-loop testing environments, enabling rapid validation and policy refinement. Physical AI Open Datasets — NVIDIA offers the most diverse large-scale, open dataset for AV that contains 1,700+ hours of driving data collected across the widest range of geographies and conditions, covering rare and complex real-world edge cases. These datasets are available on Hugging Face. Mobility leaders and industry experts, including Lucid, JLR, Uber and Berkeley DeepDrive, are showing interest in Alpamayo to develop reasoning-based AV stacks that will enable level 4 autonomy. Future models in the family will feature larger parameter counts, more detailed reasoning capabilities, more input and output flexibility, and options for commercial usage.
summary: NVIDIA releases Alpamayo family of open-source reasoning AI models and datasets for safer autonomous vehicle development with humanlike decision-making capabilities.

title: Mercedes-Benz to Deploy NVIDIA Drive AGX for Autonomous Driving
content: Nvidia announced at the Consumer Electronics Show (CES 2026) in Las Vegas that it has strengthened its partnership with German automaker Mercedes-Benz for the development of 'the world's most advanced' software-defined vehicles (SDVs). Nvidia confirmed that Mercedes-Benz will adopt its Nvidia Drive AGX system, which it said will 'enable Level 2++ driving today and pave the way for Level 4 autonomous driving in the future.' The US chipmaker also announced that its Nvidia Omniverse 'digital twins will also help Mercedes-Benz optimize vehicle design and manufacturing from factory to road.' Nvidia's Drive AV software will debut in the all-new Mercedes-Benz CLA this year, providing Level 2++ point-to-point driver assistance capabilities. The partnership has been expanded to include building passenger and cargo vans using the Nvidia Drive Hyperion system. Nvidia's Drive AV system uses an 'AI end-to-end stack for core driving, alongside a parallel classical safety stack — built on the Nvidia Halos safety system — that adds redundancy and safety guardrails. As a result, vehicles can learn from vast amounts of real and synthetic driving data to assist drivers in safely navigating complex environments and scenarios with humanlike decision-making.' Jensen Huang stated that he was 'really excited that the first vehicle using autonomous driving software developed by Nvidia will hit the road this year.' The rollout is expected to begin in the US in the first quarter of 2026, followed by Europe in the second quarter and Asia in the second half of the year. Jensen Huang said he expects that 'in the future, all cars will become autonomous and will be operated by AI. There is no doubt that autonomous vehicles will become one of the largest robotics industries.'
summary: Mercedes-Benz to deploy NVIDIA Drive AGX system in 2026 CLA model enabling Level 2++ autonomous driving with rollout across US, Europe, and Asia.

title: NVIDIA Rubin Platform: Six New Chips for Next Generation AI
content: At CES 2026, NVIDIA introduced its cutting-edge Rubin AI platform, which consists of six closely integrated chips, prominently featuring the Vera CPU and Rubin GPU. These components are now fully in production and are expected to be widely utilized by leading cloud service providers and AI research facilities starting in the latter half of 2026. The Rubin platform delivers a step-function increase in sustained throughput across pre-training, post-training, and inference. By increasing scale-up bandwidth, improving collective efficiency, and sustaining higher utilization under communication-heavy execution, Rubin raises the effective performance ceiling for large-scale training while delivering significant gains in post-training and inference workflows. The platform introduces six key components: NVIDIA Vera CPU — Designed for agentic reasoning, NVIDIA Vera is the most power-efficient CPU for large-scale AI factories. The CPU is built with 88 NVIDIA custom Olympus cores, full Armv9.2 compatibility and ultrafast NVLink-C2C connectivity. NVIDIA Rubin GPU — Featuring a third-generation Transformer Engine with hardware-accelerated adaptive compression, Rubin GPU delivers 50 petaflops of NVFP4 compute for AI inference. NVIDIA NVLink 6 Switch — Delivers the fast, seamless GPU-to-GPU communication required for today's massive MoE models. Each GPU offers 3.6TB/s of bandwidth, while the Vera Rubin NVL72 rack provides 260TB/s — more bandwidth than the entire internet. NVIDIA BlueField-4 DPU — Introduces Advanced Secure Trusted Resource Architecture (ASTRA), a system-level trust architecture that gives AI infrastructure builders a single, trusted control point. NVIDIA ConnectX-9 SuperNIC — Advanced networking interface cards for optimized data center connectivity. NVIDIA Spectrum-6 Ethernet Switch — Next generation Ethernet for AI networking, built to scale Rubin-based AI factories with higher efficiency and greater resilience.
summary: NVIDIA Rubin platform fully in production with six integrated chips delivering record performance for AI training and inference at 10x lower token cost.

title: NVIDIA Unveils Self-Driving Car Technology at CES 2026
content: Nvidia has introduced an innovative technology platform aimed at self-driving vehicles, as the leading chip manufacturer seeks to integrate AI into more tangible products. During the annual CES technology exhibition held in Las Vegas, CEO Jensen Huang announced the system, named Alpamayo, which aims to provide 'reasoning' capabilities to autonomous cars. This advancement would enable vehicles to 'navigate through unusual situations, operate safely in intricate environments, and articulate their driving choices.' Jensen Huang revealed that Nvidia is collaborating with Mercedes to develop a driverless vehicle utilizing this technology, which is expected to debut in the United States in the forthcoming months before expanding to Europe and Asia. While Nvidia's chips have been instrumental in fueling the AI surge, the spotlight has primarily been on the software they support, such as ChatGPT. However, major technology companies are increasingly focusing on hardware—specifically physical products like vehicles—where AI can be implemented. Huang, donning his signature black leather jacket, addressed a crowd of hundreds, stating that this initiative has provided Nvidia with 'vast insights' into assisting partners in constructing robotic systems. 'The moment of transformation for physical AI is nearly upon us,' Huang remarked. Analyst Paolo Pescatore from PP Foresight commented: 'Nvidia's shift towards large-scale AI and differentiating AI systems will ensure it remains significantly ahead of competitors. Alpamayo signifies a major transition for Nvidia, evolving from a primary compute provider to a platform supplier for physical AI ecosystems.' The rollout begins with US deployment in early 2026, followed by European and Asian markets later in the year.
summary: NVIDIA's Alpamayo platform brings reasoning AI to self-driving vehicles starting with Mercedes deployment in early 2026, marking shift from compute to physical AI products.

title: NVIDIA and NSF Partnership: $152 Million for Open AI Science Models
content: The U.S. National Science Foundation announced a partnership with NVIDIA to develop a set of artificial intelligence models that will transform the ability of America's scientists to leverage AI, advancing scientific discovery and ensuring U.S. leadership in AI-powered research and innovation. NSF will contribute $75 million, with NVIDIA providing an additional $77 million, to support the Open Multimodal AI Infrastructure to Accelerate Science (OMAI) project, led by the Allen Institute for AI (Ai2). The collaboration will create a fully open suite of advanced AI models specifically designed to support the U.S. scientific community. This public-private investment advances priorities set forth in the White House AI Action Plan to accelerate AI-enabled science and ensure the United States is producing the leading open models that enhance America's global AI dominance. Brian Stone, performing the duties of the NSF director, stated: 'Bringing AI into scientific research has been a game changer. NSF is proud to partner with NVIDIA to equip America's scientists with the tools to accelerate breakthroughs. These investments are not just about enabling innovation; they are about securing U.S. global leadership in science and technology and tackling challenges once thought impossible.' With this support, Ai2 will create open-source, multimodal large language models trained on scientific data and literature. These tools will enable America's researchers and developers to process and analyze research faster, generate code and visualizations, and connect new insights to past discoveries, accelerating breakthroughs across materials science, biology, energy and more. The project will provide a path forward to a future paradigm of AI-accelerated scientific discovery, supporting reliable and rigorous science and building an innovation ecosystem that benefits every American. NSF's support is provided through its Mid-Scale Research Infrastructure program. The project will also build a national AI-ready workforce, supporting training efforts that expand participation and expertise beyond traditional tech hubs, strengthening American competitiveness in AI and other critical technologies. Research teams from the University of Washington, University of Hawaii at Hilo, University of New Hampshire and University of New Mexico will participate. Michael Kratsios, director of the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy, stated: 'This partnership between NSF and NVIDIA puts the Action Plan to work and demonstrates the power of the American innovation ecosystem.'
summary: NSF and NVIDIA invest $152 million in open-source AI models for scientific research, enabling faster breakthroughs in materials science, biology, and energy.

title: Intel and NVIDIA Strategic Partnership for AI and CPUs
content: The companies announced a historic collaboration to jointly develop AI infrastructure and personal computing solutions. Intel will build NVIDIA-custom x86 CPUs that NVIDIA will integrate into its AI infrastructure platforms and offer to the market for data centers. For personal computing, Intel will build and offer to the market x86 system-on-chips (SOCs) that integrate NVIDIA RTX GPU chiplets. These new x86 RTX SOCs will power a wide range of PCs that demand integration of world-class CPUs and GPUs. The companies will focus on seamlessly connecting NVIDIA and Intel architectures using NVIDIA NVLink – integrating the strengths of NVIDIA's AI and accelerated computing with Intel's leading CPU technologies and x86 ecosystem to deliver cutting-edge solutions for customers. NVIDIA will invest $5 billion in Intel's common stock at a purchase price of $23.28 per share. The investment is subject to customary closing conditions, including required regulatory approvals. Jensen Huang, NVIDIA founder and CEO, stated: 'AI is powering a new industrial revolution and reinventing every layer of the computing stack — from silicon to systems to software. At the heart of this reinvention is NVIDIA's CUDA architecture. This historic collaboration tightly couples NVIDIA's AI and accelerated computing stack with Intel's CPUs and the vast x86 ecosystem—a fusion of two world-class platforms. Together, we will expand our ecosystems and lay the foundation for the next era of computing.' Lip-Bu Tan, CEO of Intel, added: 'Intel's x86 architecture has been foundational to modern computing for decades – and we are innovating across our portfolio to enable the workloads of the future. Intel's leading data center and client computing platforms, combined with our process technology, manufacturing and advanced packaging capabilities, will complement NVIDIA's AI and accelerated computing leadership to enable new breakthroughs for the industry. We appreciate the confidence Jensen and the NVIDIA team have placed in us with their investment and look forward to the work ahead as we innovate for customers and grow our business.'
summary: Intel and NVIDIA announce historic partnership: NVIDIA invests $5 billion in Intel to develop custom x86 CPUs and RTX GPU-integrated SOCs.

title: NVIDIA Q3 FY2026 Earnings: $57 Billion Revenue, Strong Growth
content: NVIDIA's revenues for the third quarter fiscal 2026 came in at $57 billion, up 62% year over year and 22% sequentially, according to investor reports. Jensen Huang, NVIDIA's founder and CEO, remarked that 'GPU sales are off the charts and GPUs are sold out.' The company remains optimistic about future growth, projecting revenues for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2026 to be around $65 billion, with a margin of plus or minus 2%. Moreover, NVIDIA expects ongoing profitability growth, especially since the Trump administration has authorized the company to sell H200 AI chips to select clients in China. With a competitive advantage in the AI hardware sector and anticipated increases in global data capital expenditures, NVIDIA is positioned for expansion in 2026 under the leadership of Jensen Huang. The strong performance reflects extraordinary demand for AI infrastructure across all major cloud providers and enterprises. NVIDIA's GPU business continues to benefit from the explosive growth in AI adoption and the transition to next-generation architectures like Rubin. The authorization to sell H200 chips to selected Chinese clients provides additional revenue opportunities in the strategically important Asia-Pacific region. Analysts expect the company's momentum to continue throughout 2026 as enterprises accelerate their AI deployment initiatives and cloud providers scale their data centers. The company's guidance of $65 billion in Q4 revenue underscores NVIDIA's confidence in sustained demand for its AI computing platforms.
summary: NVIDIA reports record $57B Q3 FY2026 revenue, up 62% YoY. Forecasts $65B Q4 revenue. GPUs sold out amid explosive AI demand.

title: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4.5 DLSS Announced with AI Improvements
content: At CES, NVIDIA unveiled a wave of AI performance upgrades that deliver up to 3X faster performance in creative AI apps like ComfyUI, and up to 35% more performance on LLM tools like Llama.cpp, LM Studio and Ollama. New AI models and tools accelerate AI-generated video output, and new features for NVIDIA Broadcast extend its capabilities. For AI Artists, NVIDIA introduced updates to ComfyUI that reduce VRAM usage and allow models to be offloaded into system memory, thus letting creators use larger models. These improvements represent significant advancements for content creators working with generative AI tools. The DLSS 4.5 technology incorporates hardware-accelerated adaptive compression and advanced frame generation capabilities that leverage AI to improve performance without sacrificing quality. New features in NVIDIA Broadcast enable real-time voice and video processing with AI-powered enhancements, including noise cancellation, background replacement, and virtual camera effects. The updates support a wide range of popular open-source models and tools, making advanced AI capabilities more accessible to creators and developers. NVIDIA GeForce RTX GPUs with these enhancements enable local AI inference for privacy-sensitive applications, reducing reliance on cloud services. The improvements in VRAM efficiency and model offloading capabilities enable creators to work with larger, more capable models on consumer-grade hardware. Support for emerging video models like Lightricks LTX-2 and FLUX enables creators to generate high-quality video content with unprecedented speed and efficiency. DGX Spark delivers up to 2.6x performance for large models, with new support for Lightricks LTX-2 and FLUX image models, and upcoming NVIDIA AI Enterprise availability.
summary: NVIDIA releases GeForce RTX DLSS 4.5 with up to 3X faster AI performance in creative apps and 35% improvements in LLM tools.

title: NVIDIA Siemens Partnership: Building Industrial AI Operating System
content: Siemens and NVIDIA announced a significant expansion of their strategic partnership to develop an Industrial AI Operating System. The companies aim to develop industrial and physical AI solutions that will bring AI-driven innovation to every industry and industrial workflow, as well as accelerate each other's operations. NVIDIA will provide AI infrastructure, simulation libraries, models, frameworks and blueprints, while Siemens will commit hundreds of industrial AI experts and leading hardware and software. Together, the companies aim to build the world's first fully AI-driven, adaptive manufacturing sites globally, starting in 2026 with the Siemens Electronics Factory in Erlangen, Germany. Siemens and NVIDIA will work together to build AI-accelerated industrial solutions across the full lifecycle of products and production, enabling faster innovation, continuous optimization and more resilient, sustainable manufacturing. Using an 'AI Brain' — powered by software-defined automation and industrial operations software, combined with NVIDIA Omniverse libraries and NVIDIA AI infrastructure, factories can continuously analyze their digital twins, test improvements virtually and turn validated insights into operational changes on the shopfloor. The partnership also expands GPU acceleration across Siemens' entire simulation portfolio with support for NVIDIA CUDA-X libraries and AI physics models. Siemens and NVIDIA will jointly develop a repeatable blueprint for next-generation AI factories, balancing high-density computing demands for power, cooling and automation while optimizing for both speed and efficiency. Key customers evaluating capabilities include Foxconn, HD Hyundai, KION Group and PepsiCo. By applying industrial AI operational logic to semiconductors and AI factories, the companies target 2-10x speedups in key EDA workflows including verification, layout and process optimization.
summary: NVIDIA and Siemens expand partnership to develop Industrial AI Operating System for adaptive, AI-driven manufacturing starting with Erlangen factory in 2026.

title: NVIDIA Jetson T4000: Edge AI Computing for Robotics and Autonomous Machines
content: NVIDIA introduced the NVIDIA Jetson T4000, bringing high-performance AI and real-time reasoning to a wider range of robotics and edge AI applications. The T4000 delivers up to 1,200 FP4 TFLOPs of AI compute and 64 GB of memory within a configurable 70-watt power envelope, providing an ideal balance of performance, efficiency, and scalability. Powered by NVIDIA Blackwell architecture, the Jetson T4000 supports Transformer Engine and Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) technologies. The module integrates a 12-core Arm Neoverse-V3AE CPU, three 25GbE network interfaces, and a wide range of I/O options, making it well suited for low-latency, multi-sensor, and real-time computing requirements. The T4000 features a third-generation programmable vision accelerator (PVA), dual encoders and decoders, and an optical flow accelerator for stable AI inference even under constrained compute and power budgets. The module includes 1x NVENC and 1x NVDEC hardware video codec engines, enabling real-time 4K video encoding and decoding. For system integrators, the modular architecture of Jetson T4000, combined with NVIDIA's mature software ecosystem, enables rapid integration of vision, sensing, and control systems, significantly shortening development and validation cycles. For equipment manufacturers, T4000's compact form factor and low-power design allow flexible integration into a wide range of end devices, including advanced robotics, industrial equipment, smart terminals, machine vision systems, and edge controllers. Enterprise users can deploy across diverse scenarios such as smart factories, smart retail, security, and edge sensor data processing. The module is available for order and shares the same form factor and pin compatibility with the NVIDIA Jetson T5000 module, enabling common carrier board designs.
summary: NVIDIA Jetson T4000 module delivers Blackwell-class AI performance at 1,200 FP4 TFLOPs in energy-efficient 70W package for robotics and edge AI applications.

title: NVIDIA GeForce NOW Expands to Linux PCs and Amazon Fire TV
content: NVIDIA announced major expansions to its cloud gaming service GeForce NOW, adding native applications for Linux PCs and Amazon Fire TV devices. The new native GeForce NOW app for Linux PCs, supported with Ubuntu 24.04 and later distributions, answers one of the top requests from the PC gaming community. Linux users can transform their compatible systems into GeForce RTX-powered gaming rigs, streaming supported PC titles from the cloud at up to 5K resolution and 120 fps or 1080p at 360 fps. With rendering handled in the cloud, high-end PC gaming is possible on Linux operating systems, breathing new life into older devices. Members can enjoy ray tracing, NVIDIA DLSS 4 and other RTX technologies without needing a local high-performance GPU. The app is designed to bring a seamless, native experience that fits naturally into Linux desktop workflows while giving access to the expansive GeForce NOW library. The beta release is expected early in 2026. A new native GeForce NOW app for select Amazon Fire TV sticks — starting with the Fire TV Stick 4K Plus (2nd Gen) and Fire TV Stick 4K Max (2nd Gen) — can bring RTX-powered PC gaming to another big screen in the home. Members can stream their compatible PC game libraries directly to Fire TV-connected displays to turn a compact streaming stick into a powerful cloud gaming rig. With support for gamepads and GeForce NOW's familiar interface, Fire TV users can jump into their favorite supported games without a console or gaming PC. The new apps build on GeForce NOW's existing support for Windows PCs, macOS, Chromebooks, mobile devices, smart TVs, VR devices and handhelds, powered by GeForce RTX 5080-class performance.
summary: NVIDIA launches native GeForce NOW apps for Linux PCs and Amazon Fire TV, expanding cloud gaming access to up to 5K 120fps and living room streaming.

title: NVIDIA H200 Chips: China Market Opportunity Worth $50 Billion Annually
content: Jensen Huang, NVIDIA's CEO, announced at CES 2026 that there is 'very high' demand from customers in China for the company's H200 AI chips, which the U.S. government has recently indicated it will permit for export. Huang stated that NVIDIA has resumed production of these chips and is finalizing the export license details with U.S. authorities. The H200 chips are essential for companies working on artificial intelligence models in China. Huang mentioned that 'Our supply chain is operational, and H200s are moving through our production line' during a press briefing at the CES event in Las Vegas. Investors view the Chinese market as a significant opportunity for NVIDIA, particularly as local tech firms create their own AI models. Huang has previously estimated that the market for H200 chips in China could reach a value of approximately $50 billion annually, and these potential sales are not currently included in NVIDIA's projections. He also indicated that any sales of the H200 would be in addition to the company's previously projected $500 billion forecast for the next two years. The authorization to sell H200 chips to selected Chinese clients provides additional revenue opportunities in the strategically important Asia-Pacific region. This development comes as NVIDIA continues to face geopolitical challenges but finds new opportunities to expand its presence in international markets. The company remains optimistic about capitalizing on the growing demand for advanced AI infrastructure globally.
summary: NVIDIA CEO reports 'very high' demand for H200 AI chips in China with market opportunity estimated at $50 billion annually beginning 2026.

title: NVIDIA Clara: AI-Powered Healthcare Platform for Medical Imaging and Genomics
content: NVIDIA Clara is a comprehensive, healthcare-specific AI and high-performance computing (HPC) platform designed to advance medical imaging and genomics. Clara provides robust AI-powered diagnostic tools and treatment planning solutions, significantly enhancing the accuracy and speed of medical diagnostics. The platform's capabilities include medical imaging, where AI-driven algorithms analyze medical images from various modalities such as MRI, CT, and ultrasound to detect and diagnose diseases earlier and more accurately. Clara's genomics capabilities enable large-scale genomic analysis, facilitating personalized medicine by identifying genetic markers associated with diseases and tailoring treatments accordingly. Clara features federated learning technology that allows multiple institutions to collaborate on AI model training without sharing patient data, maintaining data privacy while enhancing the model's robustness. The platform includes Clara Train, which provides APIs and toolkits to bring AI-assisted annotation capabilities to any medical viewer, with privacy-preserving federated learning capabilities. Clara Deploy provides a container-based deployment framework for AI-accelerated medical imaging workflows with features facilitating efficient memory handling, easy-to-use APIs, monitoring tools, and integration with existing services. With NVIDIA's expertise, Clara has enabled medical institutions to reduce training time from days to hours, speed up neuroimaging analysis workflows by 10X to 300X with improved accuracy, and streamline clinical radiology practices. Clara is used by leading medical institutions including Stanford, UCSF, and Memorial Sloan Kettering to transform healthcare workloads and improve diagnostic and interventional imaging capabilities.
summary: NVIDIA Clara healthcare platform enables AI-powered medical imaging and genomics analysis with federated learning for privacy-preserving collaboration across institutions.

title: NVIDIA Earth-2: Climate Digital Twin Platform for Weather and Climate Prediction
content: NVIDIA unveiled its Earth-2 platform, a groundbreaking climate digital twin cloud platform that combines AI, GPU acceleration, physical simulations and computer graphics to develop applications that can simulate and visualize weather and climate predictions at global scale with unprecedented accuracy and speed. The platform features a software stack and tools designed to enable the creation of interactive digital twins for simulating and visualizing weather, as well as delivering climate predictions at planetary scale. Central to the Earth-2 initiative is cBottle (Climate in a Bottle), the world's first generative AI foundation model designed to simulate global climate at kilometer resolution. The model can generate realistic atmospheric states conditioned on inputs like time of day, day of year and sea surface temperatures. cBottle can reduce petabytes of data by up to 3,000x for an individual weather sample, translating to a 3,000,000x data size reduction for a collection of 1,000 samples. The model was trained on high-resolution physical climate simulations and measurement-constrained estimates of observed atmospheric states over the past 50 years. cBottle can fill in missing or corrupted climate data, correct biased climate models, super-resolve low-resolution climate data and synthesize information based on patterns and previous observations. With GPU acceleration and NVIDIA's optimized Earth-2 stack, cBottle can generate climate predictions thousands of times faster than traditional numerical models without compromising accuracy. Leading scientific institutions including Max-Planck-Institute for Meteorology and Allen Institute for AI are collaborating with NVIDIA to advance climate modeling. The Max Planck Institute performed the first-ever kilometer-scale simulations of the full Earth system using Earth-2. The cBottle foundation model is available for early access, with codebase accessible from GitHub.
summary: NVIDIA Earth-2 platform with cBottle generative AI model enables kilometer-resolution climate simulation 3,000x faster than traditional physics-based models.

title: NVIDIA Nemotron: Advanced Reasoning and Multimodal AI Models
content: NVIDIA released Nemotron, an advanced reasoning and multimodal AI model family designed to enable intelligent systems to understand, reason and generate multimodal content. Nemotron models are trained on NVIDIA's supercomputers using proprietary datasets and are deployed across various applications requiring advanced reasoning capabilities. The Nemotron model family includes models of varying sizes, enabling developers to select the appropriate model for their specific use cases and computational constraints. These models excel at complex reasoning tasks, enabling them to solve problems step-by-step, generate detailed explanations for their decisions, and understand relationships between different types of data including text, images and other modalities. Nemotron models are open-source and available through Hugging Face, allowing researchers and developers worldwide to build upon and customize these models for their specific applications. The models have been designed with safety and alignment in mind, incorporating guardrails and safety measures to ensure responsible AI deployment. Nemotron enables a wide range of applications from customer service and virtual assistants to complex analytical tasks and creative content generation. The open nature of these models promotes transparency and enables the global AI community to contribute to improvements and adaptations. Companies and researchers using Nemotron can integrate these models into their existing systems and workflows, leveraging advanced multimodal reasoning capabilities without building from scratch. NVIDIA's commitment to open models reflects the company's vision of democratizing advanced AI capabilities.
summary: NVIDIA releases Nemotron advanced reasoning and multimodal AI model family open-source on Hugging Face for complex reasoning and cross-modal understanding.

title: NVIDIA DGX Spark: Accelerated Inference for Large AI Models
content: NVIDIA announced DGX Spark, delivering up to 2.6x performance gains for large model inference with new support for Lightricks LTX-2 and FLUX image models. DGX Spark is designed to accelerate inference workloads for the world's most advanced AI models, providing enterprises and AI laboratories with high-performance computing solutions for deploying generative AI applications at scale. The platform delivers significant performance improvements over previous generations, enabling faster inference speeds and lower latency for demanding applications. DGX Spark now includes support for emerging video models like Lightricks LTX-2, which enables high-quality video generation and manipulation, and FLUX image generation models for superior image synthesis capabilities. With these new model integrations, users can leverage cutting-edge generative AI models for creative and business applications with unprecedented speed and efficiency. NVIDIA AI Enterprise availability is planned for DGX Spark, providing enterprise customers with commercial support, security features, and integration tools for deploying inference workloads in production environments. The platform is particularly suited for media and entertainment companies, content creators, and enterprises requiring high-performance inference for image and video generation tasks. DGX Spark's optimizations leverage NVIDIA's software stack including TensorRT for model optimization and CUDA for GPU acceleration, ensuring maximum performance from NVIDIA GPUs. The system's architecture enables scalable deployment across multiple GPUs and nodes for handling increasingly complex models and larger inference batches. Performance improvements translate to reduced operational costs through lower energy consumption and faster time-to-inference for end-users.
summary: NVIDIA DGX Spark delivers 2.6x performance gains for large model inference with new FLUX and Lightricks LTX-2 video model support.

title: NVIDIA Cosmos: Foundational World Models for Physical AI
content: NVIDIA released Cosmos, a collection of foundational world models trained on extensive datasets that can replicate environments based on real-world physics. Cosmos models are designed to enable intelligent machines to understand, simulate and predict behavior in physical environments with high fidelity. The Cosmos model family includes multiple variants optimized for different use cases and applications, from robotics simulation to autonomous vehicle development and industrial automation. These world models are trained using vast amounts of video and sensor data, enabling them to learn the underlying physics principles governing real-world systems. With Cosmos, developers can simulate complex physical scenarios in digital environments, enabling rapid testing and validation of AI systems before deployment in the real world. The models excel at predicting future states based on current observations, enabling planning and decision-making capabilities for autonomous systems. Cosmos models are available as open-source models on Hugging Face, democratizing access to advanced world modeling capabilities for researchers and developers globally. The models support various input modalities and can work with different types of sensor data and video feeds. NVIDIA's Omniverse platform integrates with Cosmos models, enabling seamless creation of interactive digital twins for training and evaluation of AI systems. The combination of Cosmos world models with NVIDIA's simulation infrastructure provides a complete solution for developing physical AI applications. Enterprises and research institutions are leveraging Cosmos for robotics training, autonomous vehicle development, industrial process simulation, and other applications requiring accurate physical world understanding. The open-source nature of Cosmos encourages innovation and enables the broader AI community to contribute improvements and adaptations for specialized use cases.
summary: NVIDIA Cosmos foundational world models trained on vast video datasets replicate real-world physics for robotics simulation and physical AI training.

title: NVIDIA GR00T: Embodied Intelligence Model for Humanoid Robots
content: NVIDIA unveiled GR00T (Generative Robotics Object Observation Transformer), an advanced embodied intelligence model designed specifically for humanoid robots and autonomous systems. GR00T N1.6 represents NVIDIA's latest iteration of embodied intelligence, enabling robots to perceive, understand and physically interact with their environment using full-body control. The model is trained on diverse robot learning scenarios and can generalize across different robot morphologies and tasks. GR00T enables humanoid robots to develop intuitive understanding of physics, spatial relationships and object interactions, allowing them to perform complex manipulation and navigation tasks. The model integrates with NVIDIA's Jetson robotics processors and the Isaac Lab-Arena robot evaluation platform, providing a complete stack for robot development and deployment. Companies including Franka Robotics, NEURA Robotics and Humanoid are using GR00T-enabled workflows to simulate, train and validate new behaviors for robots. The model's architecture enables real-time reasoning and decision-making for physical robots, with low-latency inference suitable for time-critical applications. GR00T supports various training approaches including simulation-based training in NVIDIA Omniverse and transfer learning from simulated to real robots. The model has demonstrated strong performance across multiple benchmarks for manipulation, navigation and reasoning tasks. GR00T is available as an open-source model on Hugging Face, enabling researchers worldwide to develop advanced robotic systems. NVIDIA's ecosystem of robotics partners has adopted GR00T for developing next-generation humanoid robots for both industrial and consumer applications. The combination of GR00T with NVIDIA's full robotics stack provides developers with tools to build intelligent, autonomous systems capable of complex physical interaction with the world.
summary: NVIDIA GR00T embodied intelligence model enables humanoid robots with full-body control and intuitive environmental understanding for complex manipulation tasks.

title: OpenAI and NVIDIA Strategic Partnership: $100 Billion Investment for 10 Gigawatts AI Infrastructure
content: OpenAI and NVIDIA announced a landmark strategic partnership to deploy at least 10 gigawatts of NVIDIA systems for OpenAI's next-generation AI infrastructure. This historic collaboration will enable OpenAI to build and deploy AI datacenters with NVIDIA systems representing millions of GPUs for training and running next-generation models on the path to deploying superintelligence. To support the deployment including datacenter and power capacity, NVIDIA intends to invest up to $100 billion in OpenAI progressively as each gigawatt is deployed. The first gigawatt of NVIDIA systems is targeted to come online in the second half of 2026 using the NVIDIA Vera Rubin platform. Jensen Huang, founder and CEO of NVIDIA, stated: 'NVIDIA and OpenAI have pushed each other for a decade, from the first DGX supercomputer to the breakthrough of ChatGPT. This investment and infrastructure partnership mark the next leap forward—deploying 10 gigawatts to power the next era of intelligence.' OpenAI will work with NVIDIA as a preferred strategic compute and networking partner for its AI factory growth plans. OpenAI and NVIDIA will work together to co-optimize their roadmaps for OpenAI's model and infrastructure software and NVIDIA's hardware and software. This partnership complements the deep work OpenAI and NVIDIA are already doing with a broad network of collaborators, including Microsoft, Oracle, SoftBank, and Stargate partners, focused on building the world's most advanced AI infrastructure. The collaboration represents a significant commitment from both companies to advancing AI capabilities and scaling infrastructure globally. OpenAI has grown to over 700 million weekly active users and strong adoption across global enterprises, small businesses and developers, making this partnership critical to supporting explosive growth in AI demand and deployment.
summary: OpenAI and NVIDIA landmark partnership: NVIDIA invests $100 billion to deploy 10 gigawatts of AI infrastructure with first phase in H2 2026 using Vera Rubin.

title: NVIDIA Nokia 6G AI-RAN Partnership: $1 Billion Investment and Next-Generation Telecommunications
content: NVIDIA and Nokia announced a strategic partnership to establish the AI platform for 6G telecommunications networks. NVIDIA will invest $1 billion in Nokia at a subscription price of $6.01 per share to accelerate AI-RAN innovation. The partnership marks the beginning of the AI-native wireless era, providing the foundation to support AI-powered consumer experiences and enterprise services at the edge. The collaboration addresses the fast-growing AI-RAN market, representing a significant opportunity expected to exceed a cumulative $200 billion by 2030. NVIDIA introduces the NVIDIA Arc Aerial RAN Computer (ARC-Pro), a 6G-ready accelerated computing platform that combines connectivity, computing and sensing capabilities. The ARC-Pro reference design is available for manufacturers and network equipment providers to build commercial AI-RAN products, supporting both new buildouts and expansions to existing base stations. Nokia will accelerate the availability of its 5G and 6G RAN software on the NVIDIA CUDA platform and expand its RAN portfolio by embedding NVIDIA ARC-Pro at the core of the new AI-RAN solution. T-Mobile U.S. will collaborate with Nokia and NVIDIA to drive and test AI-RAN technologies as part of the 6G innovation and development process, with trials expected to begin in 2026 focused on field validation. Jensen Huang stated: 'Telecommunications is a critical national infrastructure — the digital nervous system of our economy and security. Built on NVIDIA CUDA and AI, AI-RAN will revolutionize telecommunications — a generational platform shift that empowers the United States to regain global leadership in this vital infrastructure technology.' Dell Technologies provides PowerEdge servers to power the new AI-RAN solution. Additional AI networking solutions cooperation includes data center switching with Nokia's SR Linux software for NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet networking platform and collaboration on Nokia's telemetry and fabric management platform for NVIDIA AI infrastructure.
summary: NVIDIA invests $1 billion in Nokia for AI-RAN 6G platform development with T-Mobile trials beginning 2026; $200B market opportunity by 2030.

title: NVIDIA G-SYNC Pulsar: Revolutionary Gaming Display Technology with 1,000+ Hz Motion Clarity
content: NVIDIA unveiled G-SYNC Pulsar, the next evolution of its groundbreaking variable refresh rate (VRR) technology for gaming displays. G-SYNC Pulsar delivers unprecedented motion clarity by combining variable frequency backlight strobing with VRR capabilities. By pulsing the display's backlight, G-SYNC Pulsar displays deliver perceived effective motion clarity of over 1,000 Hz, significantly increasing clarity and visibility of content in motion. This enables gamers to track and shoot targets with increased precision—playing at 250 frames per second delivers motion clarity effectively equivalent to a theoretical 1,000 Hz monitor. The new G-SYNC Pulsar monitors launching January 7th from Acer (Predator XB273U F5), AOC (AGON PRO AG276QSG2), ASUS (ROG Strix Pulsar XG27AQNGV), and MSI feature a 27-inch IPS display with 2560x1440 resolution and 360Hz refresh rate, with 500 nits peak brightness in HDR. Prices start at $599. Each frame is delivered with both stutter-free smoothness and motion clarity that is effectively quadruple the baseline refresh rate, even in intense fast-paced games. These are the first G-SYNC displays built in collaboration with MediaTek, enabling G-SYNC technologies to be incorporated directly into the display scaler, bypassing the need for dedicated G-SYNC modules. This streamlines production and makes G-SYNC more accessible for monitor manufacturers. G-SYNC Pulsar also includes G-SYNC Ambient Adaptive Technology, a built-in light sensor that automatically tunes color temperature and brightness based on ambient lighting in the room for optimal viewing at any hour. All four displays are capable of receiving firmware updates via micro-B USB, enabling NVIDIA to share G-SYNC Pulsar improvements with end-users. Additionally, NVIDIA validated 63 new G-SYNC Compatible displays, including Samsung's new Odyssey G60H, the world's first 1,040Hz gaming monitor, and ASUS ROG Strix 5K XG27JCG with 5120x2880 resolution.
summary: NVIDIA G-SYNC Pulsar debuts January 7 with 1,000+ Hz effective motion clarity; first collaborative displays with MediaTek; Ambient Adaptive Technology included.

title: NVIDIA DLSS 4.5: Advanced Super Resolution and 6X Dynamic Multi Frame Generation for RTX 50 Series
content: NVIDIA announced DLSS 4.5, a major upgrade to its neural rendering technology suite featuring a second-generation transformer model for Super Resolution and groundbreaking frame generation capabilities. DLSS 4.5 introduces Dynamic Multi Frame Generation, a new 6X Multi Frame Generation mode, and a second-generation transformer model for DLSS Super Resolution with state-of-the-art image quality. The 6X Dynamic Multi Frame Generation mode automatically adjusts frame generation techniques to maximize smoothness and performance, capable of generating up to five additional AI-rendered frames for every single traditionally rendered frame. This enables 4K 240Hz path-traced gaming on GeForce RTX 50 Series GPUs with fully ray-traced visuals at competitive esports framerates exceeding 240+ FPS. DLSS 4.5 Super Resolution is available to all GeForce RTX GPU owners, including RTX 20, 30, 40, and 50 Series cards. Users can access the feature through the NVIDIA App by installing the latest beta update, with official release scheduled for January 13. Dynamic Multi Frame Generation and the 6X Multi Frame Generation mode are exclusive to GeForce RTX 50 Series GPUs and will roll out in Spring 2026. Over 250 games and applications already support DLSS 4 technology with Multi Frame Generation, and highly anticipated titles are adding support including 007 First Light, Phantom Blade Zero, PRAGMATA with path tracing, Resident Evil Requiem, and more. The technology represents a leap in AI-powered graphics rendering, combining transformer-based Super Resolution with advanced frame synthesis for unprecedented gaming performance and visual quality. NVIDIA's DLSS 4 has already become the company's most rapidly adopted gaming technology since its debut at CES 2025.
summary: NVIDIA DLSS 4.5 features second-gen transformer Super Resolution and 6X Dynamic Multi Frame Generation; 250+ games supported; RTX 50 exclusive features Spring 2026.

title: NVIDIA RTX AI PCs: Local LLM Inference and Privacy-Preserving On-Device AI Processing
content: NVIDIA is driving the expansion of RTX AI PCs, enabling advanced AI inference directly on consumer hardware without reliance on cloud services. RTX AI PCs leverage NVIDIA's optimized software stack including TensorRT for RTX, CUDA, and DLSS 4 to enable local execution of sophisticated AI models including large language models, image generators, and multimodal AI applications. The platform addresses growing demand for generative AI computing at the edge while maintaining data privacy and reducing latency associated with cloud-based inference. TensorRT for RTX is a GPU-accelerated inference backend specifically optimized for consumer and developer devices with RTX graphics cards. It performs just-in-time compilation and dynamic optimization of AI models for specific RTX GPU architectures, including CUDA core and Tensor Core utilization. According to NVIDIA, TensorRT for RTX enables up to twice the inference performance compared to Microsoft's DirectML framework. Aggressive optimizations also reduce model sizes by up to 70 percent, making large language models and multimodal models practical for local execution. Open-source LLMs such as GPT-OSS-20B demonstrate that 20 billion-parameter models can run efficiently on modern consumer and professional RTX GPUs with expert-tuned kernels like FlashAttention and Triton. These optimizations reduce memory footprints, improve time-to-first-token, and decrease latency during inference. RTX AI PCs support local execution of AI models from Stable Diffusion-class image generators to large language models, enabling developers to run private assistants, offline-capable applications, and creative workloads without cloud round-trips. The platform is compatible with RTX graphics cards from Turing generation (RTX 20 Series) onwards. NVIDIA's software ecosystem provides containerized deployment through OCI-compliant formats like Docker and Podman, supporting both Windows and Linux systems. Privacy-preserving local inference addresses growing concerns about data security and enables organizations to maintain control over sensitive prompts and outputs.
summary: NVIDIA RTX AI PCs enable local LLM and image model inference with TensorRT for RTX achieving 2X performance vs DirectML; 70% model size reduction.

title: NVIDIA ACE: Autonomous Game Characters with Advanced AI and Small Language Models
content: NVIDIA expanded its ACE (Autonomous Creatures Engine) technology for gaming, introducing autonomous game characters that perceive, plan, and act like human players using advanced AI. ACE is a suite of RTX-accelerated digital human technologies that bring game characters to life with generative AI. NVIDIA is now expanding ACE from conversational NPCs to fully autonomous game characters using new small language models (SLMs) capable of planning at human-like frequencies required for realistic decision-making. Enabling autonomous characters are new ACE small language models, capable of planning at human-like frequencies for realistic decision making and multi-modal SLMs for vision and audio that allow AI characters to hear audio cues and perceive their environment. These models enable living, dynamic game worlds with companions that comprehend and support player goals, and enemies that adapt dynamically to player tactics. The first digital human technology on-device model is called Nemotron-4 4B Instruct, designed for role-playing with leading retrieval-augmented generation and function-calling capabilities, allowing game characters to respond to gamers more intuitively and perform accurate relevant actions. NVIDIA is partnering with leading game developers to incorporate ACE autonomous game characters into their titles. Game publishers including Perfect World Games are advancing ACE demonstrations with new AI-powered vision capabilities, enabling characters to see and identify people and objects in the real world using the computer's camera, adding augmented reality layers to gameplay experiences. Applications include conversational NPCs that hold intelligent conversations with players, AI companions that support player goals, and enemies that adapt to player tactics dynamically. Total War: PHARAOH will receive an ACE tech demo showing how AI assists players in navigating complex game systems and mechanics. PUBG: Battlegrounds' Ally update (coming H1 2026) adds long-term memory to evolve AI teammate intelligence and capability, powered by NVIDIA ACE. Mecha BREAK showcases ACE and digital human technologies, bringing characters to life with dynamic and immersive gameplay. ACE microservices are available as NVIDIA NIM microservices—easy to deploy, highly performant, and optimized to run on NVIDIA Graphics Delivery Network or NVIDIA RTX AI PCs.
summary: NVIDIA ACE autonomous characters use small language models for human-like planning and multi-modal perception; PUBG and Total War integrations in 2026.

title: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 50 Series: Blackwell Architecture Gaming GPUs with AI Horsepower
content: NVIDIA announced the GeForce RTX 50 Series graphics cards powered by the Blackwell architecture, bringing game-changing AI capabilities to gamers and creators. The RTX 50 Series lineup includes the RTX 5090, RTX 5080, RTX 5070 Ti, RTX 5070, RTX 5060 Ti, and RTX 5060 models. The RTX 5090 is the most powerful GeForce GPU ever made, featuring 32 GB of super-fast GDDR7 memory and delivering unprecedented AI horsepower for both gaming and creative workloads. All RTX 50 Series cards are powered by NVIDIA Blackwell architecture featuring fifth-generation Tensor Cores for maximum AI performance with FP4 and DLSS 4 support. The architecture includes new streaming multiprocessors optimized for neural shaders and fourth-generation ray tracing cores built for Mega Geometry. These GPUs enable full ray tracing with neural rendering powered by advanced Tensor Cores, supporting breakthrough graphics technologies including DLSS 4 with Multi Frame Generation for unprecedented frame rates and visual quality. RTX 50 Series enables new experiences including AI-enhanced graphics and performance, game-winning responsiveness through NVIDIA Reflex 2 with Frame Warp for reduced latency, and true-to-life graphics with full ray tracing and neural rendering. NVIDIA DLSS 4 Multi Frame Generation enables gamers to achieve 100+ frames per second in demanding games, with DLSS 4.5 improving image quality and frame pacing further. RTX 50 Series also supports NVIDIA ACE for digital humans and AI assistants, as well as NVIDIA Broadcast and ninth-generation NVIDIA Encoder for video enhancement and streaming. The GPUs deliver up to 21,760 CUDA cores and support PCIe 5.0 interface and DisplayPort connections. NVIDIA manufactured these cards through TSMC on a custom 4N process node. RTX 50 Series supports various gaming and creative applications including NVIDIA Studio creator tools and NVIDIA app for Game Ready and Studio drivers, providing a comprehensive platform for gaming, content creation, and AI workloads.
summary: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 50 Series powered by Blackwell: up to 21,760 CUDA cores, GDDR7 memory, fifth-gen Tensor Cores for AI-enhanced gaming and creation.

title: NVIDIA RTX Remix: Dynamic Graphics Effects and Enhanced Modding Tools
content: NVIDIA announced RTX Remix updates introducing a new logic system for dynamic graphics effects in games. RTX Remix is NVIDIA's game modding framework that enables developers and modders to bring advanced ray-traced graphics to classic games. The new logic system enhances the modding tools by enabling more sophisticated dynamic graphics effects that respond to game events and player actions. This advancement makes it easier for the modding community to create visually enhanced versions of older games with ray tracing, neural rendering, and other advanced graphics technologies. RTX Remix supports DLSS technology integration, allowing modded games to benefit from DLSS 4 Multi Frame Generation and other performance-enhancing features. The platform democratizes access to advanced graphics technologies, enabling indie developers and modding communities to create professional-quality enhanced versions of classic games without extensive graphics programming expertise. The logic system enables event-based dynamic graphics effects that automatically trigger based on game states, player interactions, and environmental conditions, providing more immersive and responsive visual experiences. RTX Remix has been used successfully to enhance numerous classic games with modern ray-traced graphics while maintaining the original gameplay experience. The framework supports various game engines and modding approaches, providing flexibility for creative developers. By lowering barriers to entry for advanced graphics implementation, RTX Remix expands the ecosystem of ray-traced and AI-enhanced games available to players.
summary: NVIDIA RTX Remix adds dynamic graphics effects logic system enabling sophisticated modded ray-traced games with DLSS 4 integration for enhanced classic titles.

title: NVIDIA RTX 50 Series Gaming: 250+ Games with DLSS 4 Support and Major AAA Titles
content: NVIDIA's GeForce RTX 50 Series gaming ecosystem has reached critical mass with over 250 games and applications supporting DLSS 4 technology with Multi Frame Generation. This represents the most rapidly adopted gaming technology in NVIDIA's history. Major AAA titles are adding RTX integrations including 007 First Light with DLSS 4 Multi Frame Generation for PC release, PRAGMATA with path tracing and DLSS 4, Resident Evil Requiem launching with path-traced DLSS 4-accelerated features, Phantom Blade Zero with ray tracing and DLSS 4, Active Matter with DLSS 4 Multi Frame Generation and ray tracing, Screamer with DLSS 4 Multi Frame Generation, and DEFECT with DLSS 4 Multi Frame Generation. All major gaming publishers and developers are integrating NVIDIA RTX technologies into their upcoming releases, with emphasis on leveraging DLSS 4 Multi Frame Generation for superior performance and visual quality. The breadth of game support demonstrates strong industry alignment with NVIDIA's RTX and DLSS technologies. Games are leveraging RTX features for ray tracing, neural rendering, DLSS 4 frame generation, and NVIDIA Reflex 2 for competitive gaming responsiveness. The ecosystem expansion continues with new announcements of RTX integrations in 2026 releases. NVIDIA's annual driver cadence ensures continuous optimization for new games and hardware. The partnership ecosystem includes major game engines like Unreal Engine 5 and others, making RTX integration accessible to developers globally. DLSS 4's rapid adoption across 250+ titles demonstrates that AI-powered frame generation has become essential for modern PC gaming, enabling smooth, high-quality experiences at high resolutions and refresh rates.
summary: NVIDIA RTX 50 Series: 250+ games support DLSS 4; major AAA titles like 007 First Light, Resident Evil Requiem adding ray tracing and frame generation.

title: NVIDIA Digital Humans and MetaHuman Creation: AI-Powered Avatar Technology for Games and Virtual Production
content: NVIDIA advanced its digital human technology ecosystem enabling developers to create lifelike AI-powered avatars for gaming and virtual production applications. Digital humans created with NVIDIA ACE technology combine character sensing, intelligence, animation, and voice synthesis capabilities into end-to-end solutions. The foundation for digital humans includes MetaHuman avatars from Unreal Engine, which provide pre-rigged, realistic characters ready for animation and AI integration. NVIDIA provides comprehensive technical support and tooling for piecing together different AI frameworks to create believable digital avatars without requiring extensive graphics programming expertise. Key pillars of NVIDIA's digital human platform include audio capture and processing for character comprehension, intelligence systems using large and small language models with retrieval-augmented generation capabilities, animation systems for expressing character emotions and actions, and voice synthesis for natural speech output. NVIDIA ACE integrates these components into a unified microservice architecture, making it practical to deploy digital humans in various contexts including customer service applications, humanoid robotics for virtual factories, virtual presence applications, and AI NPCs in gaming environments. Digital humans can store long-term memory about player interactions, enabling evolution of character personality and behavior over time. Function-calling capabilities enable digital humans to trigger dynamic state changes and actions in virtual environments in real-time. NVIDIA's Graphics Delivery Network provides low-latency processing of digital human workloads across 100+ countries, while RTX AI PCs enable local execution of lighter digital human models for privacy-sensitive applications. Developers can leverage NVIDIA's domain-specific AI workflows for next-generation interactive avatars tailored to specific use cases. The vision Hawk's focus on creating AI agents that exist autonomously in virtual worlds, capable of planning and acting outside turn-by-turn conversation paradigms, driving dynamic narratives and interactive experiences.
summary: NVIDIA digital humans technology integrates ACE, MetaHumans, LLMs with memory, animation, and voice for autonomous game characters and virtual production.
